title: Splat Bridge XR
description: A web console to generate Gaussian-splat worlds into a Unity XR session over WebSocket.

sections.text: Gaussian splats have opened the door for generation of full 3D worlds but the process of iterating on generated assets in XR is a challenge.
How do we control and update spatial environments in real time?
Splat Bridge XR is a system that enables live generation and in-session streaming of AI-generated 3D environments into XR.

This system enables live generation and in-session streaming of AI-generated 3D environments into XR. A user can draft a prompt using World Labs Marble, automatically download the result as a Gaussian splat, and load that world inside an active XR session without leaving the session.

The system uses WebSockets to enable real-time, event-driven updates between the bridge and Unity, allowing new worlds to load instantly without restarting the XR session. Gaussian splats (SPZ) were chosen for their fast, photorealistic rendering, while a local bridge architecture minimizes latency and complexity; together with a phone-first control UI, this creates a lightweight, multi-device system where XR is immersive and the web acts as a live control layer.

If extended, this system could evolve into a full spatial scene director platform with multi-user collaborative world editing, natural-language scene control, and integration with simulation or robotics systems, supported by persistent world state management.
